title: More on cubic K3 categories
speaker: Daniel Huybrechts
tags: Workshop on Chow Groups Motives and Derived Categories

proof_stages.label: K3-category framework and numerical lattice
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines a K3 category as a k-linear triangulated Calabi-Yau-2 category, poses the equivalence and autoequivalence problems, and introduces the numerical Grothendieck group with its symmetric Euler form and signature as a basic invariant.

proof_stages.label: K3-surface Torelli and derived autoequivalence paradigm
proof_stages.summary: Classical and derived Torelli theorems for K3 surfaces are reviewed, together with Hodge-theoretic descriptions of automorphisms, derived autoequivalences, spherical twists, and Bridgeland's conjectural description of the relevant autoequivalence group.

proof_stages.label: Twisted K3 comparison categories
proof_stages.summary: Twisted K3 categories are set up via twisted sheaves and B-field Hodge structures, their derived Torelli theorem is recalled, and the crucial simplification is that when there are no (-2)-classes or spherical objects the kernel of the cohomological autoequivalence action reduces to the double shift.

proof_stages.label: Cubic Kuznetsov component and its Hodge surrogate
proof_stages.summary: For a smooth cubic fourfold X, the Kuznetsov component A_X is defined as the orthogonal complement of O_X,O_X(1),O_X(2), its rationality and period-theoretic motivations are given, and Addington-Thomas's integral Hodge structure is used to formulate Torelli and symplectic-autoequivalence analogues for cubic K3 categories.

proof_stages.label: Generalized K3 periods and cubic period embedding
proof_stages.summary: The Mukai lattice period domain is introduced, ordinary and twisted K3 periods are distinguished from non-torsion B-field and purely symplectic periods, and a primitive A_2 embedding identifies cubic periods as a codimension-two locus whose induced Hodge structures are either twisted projective K3 type or symplectic type.

proof_stages.label: Twisted-K3 realization criterion for cubic categories
proof_stages.summary: Using Hassett-type Noether-Lefschetz divisors and a prime-factor numerical condition, the speaker states and sketches the proof that generically A_X is equivalent to a twisted K3 derived category exactly on the twisted K3 period locus, with necessity from Fourier-Mukai-induced Hodge isometries and sufficiency from plane cubics plus deformation of kernels.

proof_stages.label: Very general symplectic autoequivalence computation
proof_stages.summary: The symplectic autoequivalence group of the Kuznetsov component of a very general cubic is stated to be infinite cyclic, and the proof is sketched by combining the no-(-2)-classes case of the twisted K3 autoequivalence theorem with an explicit ever-present autoequivalence, describable through matrix factorizations.

examples.name: Kleinian-resolution negative-definite K3 category

examples.engagement_reason: The speaker names the Kleinian-resolution construction and states that the supported-sheaf category has negative-definite form, without computing or checking it.
